signal_title: Online Safety Amendment (Social Media Minimum Age) Act 2024
risk_rationale: The new Australian legislation prohibits individuals under 16 from holding accounts, conflicting with TikTok's current policy allowing 13+ account holders. This presents a material compliance gap requiring immediate product changes to prevent platform non-compliance and potential legal action.

compliance_gaps.gap_description: The new regulation requires a minimum age of 16 for account holders, conflicting with TikTok's current minimum age of 13.

remediations.title: Adjust Minimum Age for Account Creation in Australia
remediations.description: Update the account creation process to enforce a minimum age of 16 for users in Australia, including verification mechanisms to prevent underage account creation.
acceptance_criteria: Account creation is blocked for users under 16 in Australia
Age verification process is implemented to ensure compliance

business_impact: Potential loss of user base in Australia for the 13-15 age group, impacting engagement metrics and advertising revenue from this demographic.
recommended_actions: Implement age verification mechanisms for new account registrations in Australia.
Communicate changes to existing users and update terms of service.
Monitor compliance and user feedback post-implementation.
